name: Faramarz
description: Faramarz is a renowned hero in Persian mythology, celebrated for his exceptional strength, unwavering loyalty, and unwavering commitment to justice. He is the son of Rostam, the most celebrated hero of Persia, and thus inherits a legacy of courage and might. Faramarz is often depicted as a valiant warrior, skilled in swordsmanship and horsemanship, and a protector of the innocent. His adventures are filled with epic battles against formidable foes, both human and supernatural, all undertaken in the name of defending the Persian kingdom and upholding righteousness. Unlike his father, his stories are less prominent in the Shahnameh, but he is still known as a significant figure. He is primarily known for his campaign to conquer and convert the Gangdiz region to Zoroastrianism, displaying his unwavering dedication to his faith and his kingdom. Faramarz's character embodies the ideal of the righteous warrior, demonstrating the importance of duty, honor, and unwavering resolve in the face of adversity. While not as ubiquitous as his father, his tales highlight the strength and piety expected of Persian heroes. He represents a steadfast figure in Persian lore, a symbol of courage and faith. Faramarz is a testament to the enduring power of heroic ideals in Persian culture.
subtitle: Valiant Son of Rostam

keyMyths.title: Conquest of Gangdiz
keyMyths.description: Faramarz leads a campaign to conquer Gangdiz, a region known for its sorcerers and evil practices. He defeats the sorcerers, converts the people to Zoroastrianism, and brings the region under Persian rule. This highlights his religious zeal and military prowess.
keyMyths.source: Various epic poems and oral traditions

keyMyths.title: Inheriting Rostam's Legacy
keyMyths.description: As the son of Rostam, Faramarz carries the weight of his father's legendary status. He strives to live up to this legacy by demonstrating his own courage, skill, and dedication to justice. He proves himself a worthy heir to Rostam's fame.

keyMyths.title: Battles Against Demons
keyMyths.description: Faramarz faces numerous supernatural foes, including demons and evil spirits, throughout his adventures. He relies on his strength, faith, and the blessings of Ahura Mazda to overcome these challenges. This shows his moral fortitude.

sources.author: Ferdowsi
sources.description: The primary source for Persian mythology, although Faramarz's role is less prominent than Rostam's.
sources.text: The primary source for Persian mythology, although Faramarz's role is less prominent than Rostam's.

sources.author: Various
sources.description: Stories about Faramarz have been passed down through generations in Persian folklore.
sources.text: Stories about Faramarz have been passed down through generations in Persian folklore.
